# Only Once Share — ooshare.io

> Free, open-source, end-to-end encrypted secret sharing tool.

Only Once Share lets you send passwords, API keys, and sensitive information securely via one-time self-destructing links. Secrets are encrypted in the browser using AES-256-GCM before transmission — the server never sees plaintext data.

## Key Facts

- **Encryption**: AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA-256 key derivation (client-side, Web Crypto API)
- **Architecture**: Zero-knowledge — the server stores only encrypted blobs
- **Pricing**: Completely free, no limits, no account required
- **License**: MIT open source (https://github.com/dhdtech/only-once-share)
- **Deployment**: Cloud at ooshare.io or self-hosted via Docker
- **Languages**: English, Chinese, Spanish, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese
- **TTL Options**: 1, 4, 12, 24, 48, or 72 hours
- **URL Format**: 8-character base62 short aliases

## How It Works

1. User enters secret text in the browser
2. Browser generates a random AES-256-GCM key via Web Crypto API
3. Secret is encrypted entirely client-side; key is placed in URL fragment (#)
4. Only the encrypted blob is sent to the server and stored in Redis with a TTL
5. A one-time link is generated containing the encrypted secret ID and the key in the fragment
6. When the recipient opens the link, the encrypted blob is retrieved and atomically deleted (Redis GETDEL)
7. The browser decrypts the secret using the key from the URL fragment
8. The secret can never be viewed again

## What Makes It Different

- True zero-knowledge: encryption key never leaves the browser (URL fragments are never sent to servers)
- Completely free with no artificial limits (unlike OneTimeSecret's 100KB cap or Password.link's 8/month)
- Open source MIT license — fully auditable code
- 6-language support for global accessibility
- No account required — zero friction

## Links

- Website: https://ooshare.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/dhdtech/only-once-share
- Security Details: https://ooshare.io/security
- Blog: https://ooshare.io/blog
